title: grok-test.yaml
description: Official JSON Schema for grok-test.yaml — defines AI-powered test suites for code quality, security, accessibility, and performance audits triggered via @grok. Compatible with grok-install.yaml@1.0+ and grok@2026.4+.

version.description: Semantic version of this test configuration file.

author.description: X handle of the test config owner, prefixed with '@'.

compatibility.description: Platform specs this file is tested and compatible with.

test_suites.description: Named test suite definitions. Each key is the suite identifier used in '@grok test <Name>' triggers. Use 'all' as the key to run all suites.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this test suite evaluates.

prompt.description: Instruction prompt sent to Grok as the evaluation directive. Be specific about what constitutes a pass or fail.

files.description: Glob patterns selecting files to include in this test suite's scope. Omit to test the entire repository.

exclude_files.description: Glob patterns for files to exclude from the suite scope even if matched by the files patterns.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature for evaluation responses. Low values (0.1–0.3) produce more consistent, deterministic reviews.

alert_level.description: Minimum severity level at which Grok should raise an alert or fail the suite.

fail_on.description: Severity threshold at or above which the suite is marked as failed. 'error' only fails on critical issues; 'warning' is stricter.

max_findings.description: Maximum number of findings to report per run. Useful for high-noise suites in large codebases.

categories.description: Test categories applied by this suite. Used for filtering and reporting.

block_merge_on_fail.description: When true, a failing suite will block PR merges until issues are resolved.

enabled.description: Whether this suite is active. Set to false to disable without removing the definition.
